narrative: Antonio Tejero Molina, a Spanish colonel who led a failed military coup attempt in 1981, has died at age 93 [1][3]. Tejero was the central figure in the February 23, 1981 coup attempt, during which he held Spain's Parliament hostage for 18 hours before surrendering when it became clear he lacked support from the country's armed forces [1][2]. The revolt raised fears of a return to Francoist fascism in Spain [3]. Notably, Tejero's death occurred on the same day that the Spanish government declassified documents related to the 1981 coup attempt [3].

citations.title: Antonio Tejero Molina, 93, Dies; Spanish Colonel Led Failed Coup
citations.quote: He held Spain's Parliament hostage for 18 hours on Feb. 23, 1981, before surrendering after it became clear that he had little support from the country's armed forces.
